topic.what: Trustless identity authentication and dynamic state verification for AI agents using decentralized identifiers and verifiable credentials
topic.how: Leverages W3C DIDs/VCs for self-managed identities plus challenge-response mechanism (readiness probe + context consistency check) to verify dynamic execution state at interaction time
topic.why_matters: AI agents lack proper IAM mechanisms; traditional systems assume human users/persistent identifiers; enables secure A2A interactions in large-scale decentralized ecosystems

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total end-to-end latency for complete protocol (identity auth + state verification)
characteristics.evidence: Figure 6 shows stable ~13.5s across 1v1 to 50v50 concurrency
characteristics.context: Sepolia testnet; identity auth ~6.5s, readiness probe ~6s, context check <1s

characteristics.what_it_measures: System throughput of concurrent authentication sessions
characteristics.evidence: Figure 8 shows linear scaling from 0.07 TPS (1v1) to 3.25 TPS (50v50)

characteristics.what_itasures: Financial cost for on-chain DID registration
characteristics.evidence: Table II; gas=58,238 units at 4.88 Gwei
Section VI-B: 'average storage overhead per VC is 1.23 KB'
characteristics.context: W3C VC data model compliant credentials

characteristics.what_it_measures: Latency for computing SHA-256 hash over session context
characteristics.evidence: Figure 7; linear scaling R2=0.9996; ~400ms for 40MB context

characteristics.what_it_measures: On-chain gas units consumed for DID registration
characteristics.context: Ethereum Sepolia testnet; ERC-1056 registry contract

good_for: dynamic capability verification at interaction time

novelty: First framework addressing dynamic state verification for AI agents in decentralized settings
Challenge-response mechanism combining readiness probe and context consistency check
Fine-grained custom VC types for agent-specific attributes (provenance, capabilities, compliance)
Security proofs under EUF-CMA signature assumptions for identity and state forgery
Experimental evaluation on Ethereum Sepolia testnet with up to 50 concurrent agent pairs
open_problems: Privacy preservation during repeated A2A interactions
Improving throughput beyond 3.25 TPS for production-scale deployments
Handling privacy vs verifiability tradeoff in state verification
Integration with additional blockchain networks beyond ERC-1056
